title: Symmetry and integrability in the anyon-Hubbard model
authors: Martin Bonkhoff, Grennon Gurney, Friethjof Theel, Peter Schmelcher, Nathan L. Harshman, Thore Posske
abstract: Recent cold atom experiments have realized one-dimensional anyons and enabled the tuning of 1D~statistics between bosons and fermions. Here, we analyze the symmetries, integrability, and resulting degeneracies of the underlying anyon-Hubbard model of finite length. Our results reveal a switching between symmetry classes AI, BDI, and CI in dependence on system size, particle number, and boundary conditions, and show that two anyons with periodic boundaries are integrable, while two anyons with open boundary conditions are not. We include a comprehensive analysis of all model limits, especially of interacting bosons and pseudofermions and resolve spectral signatures. We additionally reveal an exactly solvable doublon state that hides in the continuum of scattering states and the exact solution of the nullspace of two noninteracting anyons. The uncovered symmetries shape the fundamental properties of the one-dimensional anyons at hand, and the predicted states are accessible in state-of-the-art experiments.
summary: This work provides a comprehensive theoretical study of the anyon-Hubbard model, determining how its symmetries and integrability are governed by boundary conditions and particle statistics. Key findings show that periodic boundaries preserve integrability, while open boundaries generally break it. This has implications for understanding exotic quantum phases realized in cold atom experiments.
main_problem: The paper analyzes the symmetries, integrability, and resulting degeneracies of the anyon-Hubbard model, which describes one-dimensional anyons.
main_result: The integrability of the system depends critically on boundary conditions: two anyons are integrable with periodic boundaries but generally not with open boundaries.
method: The analysis combines symmetry classification using point groups (AI, BDI, CI) with techniques like the Bethe ansatz and character theory to determine spectral properties.
model_or_system: The model is the anyon-Hubbard model, characterized by a statistical parameter $\theta$ that tunes statistics between bosons ($\theta=0$) and fermions ($\theta=\pi$). It is studied on a finite lattice of length $L$ with interactions $U$ and tunneling $J$.
key_observables: Symmetry classes (AI, BDI, CI), level spacing statistics (Poissonian vs. Wigner-Dyson), and the existence of exactly solvable states (e.g., doublon state).
important_parameters: System size ($L$), particle number ($N$), statistical parameter ($\theta$), and boundary conditions (Open vs. Periodic).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on mapping the anyonic system to an effective bosonic model and applying advanced group theory (dihedral groups) to calculate state degeneracies.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the model setup, parameter space, and level statistics, showing distinct behavior for periodic versus open boundary conditions.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes symmetries (using point groups and character theory), then moves to integrability diagnostics via level spacing, and finally provides explicit analytical solutions for small particle numbers.
why_interesting: The explicit connection between topological statistics (anyons), symmetries, and integrability provides deep insights into the quantum dynamics of strongly interacting, non-standard quantum matter systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) The anyon-Hubbard model describes N anyons on L sites with creation operators ˆa† j, equivalent to interacting bosons with density-dependent Peierls phase. (b) Its flat parameter space (red) consists of the Hubbard interaction U and the cyclic statistical parameter θ, realizing bosons for θ = 0 and pseudofermions for θ = π. For open boundary conditions in the Tonks-Girardeau limit of infinitely strong interactions, all particles become the same (tip of the conic parameter space). Periodic boundary conditions prevent this identification (cylindric parameter space), see Eq. (4).

FIG. 2. The two-particle anyon-Hubbard model’s integrability depends on its parameters and boundary conditions. (a) and (d) depict the energy levels for parameters N = 2, L = 6, and U/J = 1. (a) For open boundaries, the spectrum exhibits avoided level crossings, see insets (b) and (c), a signature of nonintegrability. (d) For periodic boundaries, the line colors indicate angular momenta q according to Sec. III B. Crossing between states with different angular momenta is expected (e), whereas crossings in the same angular momentum sector (f) are indicative of additional exactly-solvable states. (g) and (h-l) depict the symmetry-reduced energy level spacing statistics, see main text for...
